summary: KOSPI faces significant volatility amid Middle East tensions; slid near 5,409.17 points. Bank of Korea nominee Shin Hyun-song signals hawkish stance. Retail investors remain active buyers despite negative returns.
market_drivers: Geopolitical Risks: Middle East conflict (Iran-Israel focus) spiking energy costs and volatility
Market Performance: KOSPI dropped 6% to 5,409.17, worst performer in Asia; -13% since Feb 27
BOK Policy: Hawkish outlook from incoming Governor Shin Hyun-song; concerns over household debt
